message:
feat(demo_showcase): auto-cap ultra-wide terminals + log message truncation

Rendering improvements for wide terminals and log readability:

Ultra-wide terminal handling:
- Auto-cap layout width at 200 columns when no explicit --max-width is
  set, preventing layout breakage on ultra-wide monitors
- apply_pty_width_cap() uses `stty cols` to align PTY driver line
  wrapping with the app's capped width, fixing cursor positioning
  artifacts when terminal width exceeds the layout width
- Set terminal_width = capped_width so padding fills the layout
  uniformly rather than trying to span the full physical terminal
- Add crossterm dependency for crossterm::terminal::size() detection

Rendering pipeline fix:
- Move pad_to_terminal_width() from the Model::view() trait impl to a
  standalone method on App, keeping trait impl focused on view logic
- Extract pad_lines_to_width() as a free function to pad every rendered
  line to uniform width before terminal padding, eliminating the
  "slanted" ragged-right appearance

Log message truncation:
- Add LogColumnWidths.message field (Option<usize>) to control max
  message column width
- LogFormatter::format_message() now truncates with Unicode-aware width
  calculation and appends ellipsis (…) when text exceeds budget
- LogsPage::format_logs() computes message_width from remaining space
  after fixed columns (timestamp + level + target + spacing)
- All existing tests updated to include message: None defaults

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -45,6 +45,9 @@ serde_json.workspace = true
 # Async runtime
 tokio = { version = "1.40", features = ["full"] }
 
+# Terminal handling
+crossterm.workspace = true
+
 # Utilities
 unicode-width.workspace = true
 chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }

@@ -789,6 +789,7 @@ mod tests {
             animations: AnimationMode::Reduced,
             mouse: true,
             alt_screen: false,
+            max_width: None,
             seed: Some(12345),
             files_root: Some(PathBuf::from("/data")),
             self_check: true,
